name: Stack
desc: An adapter class that provides a Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) data structure.

name: Stack.from
desc:
Creates a stack containing the elements of the given iterable.
Elements are pushed in the order they appear, so the last element
of the iterable will be at the top of the stack.

name: Stack.new
desc: Creates an empty stack using a Deque as the default underlying container.

name: clear
desc: Clears the stack.

name: empty
desc: Returns true if the stack is empty.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: isNotEmpty
desc: Returns true if the stack has at least one element.

name: iterator
desc: Returns an iterator that iterates from the top of the stack to the bottom (LIFO order).

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: pop
desc:
Removes and returns the top element from the stack.
Throws a StateError if the stack is empty.

name: push
desc: Pushes an element onto the top of the stack.

name: size
desc: Returns the number of elements in the stack.

name: swap
desc: Exchanges the contents of this stack with those of other.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: top
desc:
Returns the top element of the stack without removing it.
Throws a StateError if the stack is empty.

name: StdException

desc: Creates an exception with the given message.

name: toString

name: StdRandom
desc: A deterministic, predictable C++ style pseudo-random number generator.

name: StdRandom.new
desc: Natively constructs a new random generator.

name: currentSeed
desc: Returns the current seed, if one was explicitly bound.

name: flush
desc: Flushes (advances and discards) the next count states dynamically.

desc:
A statically global, easily accessible random generator cleanly replicating
global rand() usage without needing explicit instance allocations.

desc: Statically grabs a globally bounded uniform random integer instantly.

desc: Statically reseeds the globally shared StdRandom instance dynamically.

desc: Generates the next random integer from 0 to 2^32 - 1.

name: nextBool
desc: Generates a random boolean explicitly modeling a coin-flip.

name: nextDouble
desc: Generates a strictly bounded random double mathematically between 0.0 and 1.0.

name: range
desc: Generates a strictly bounded random integer between min (inclusive) and max (exclusive).

name: seed
desc: Re-seeds the internal generator precisely.

name: StdRangeError

name: StringView
desc: A non-owning, zero-allocation reference to a string or a contiguous substring.

desc: Creates a StringView from an entire String.

name: StringView.substring
desc: Creates a StringView from a specific segment of a String.

name: contains
desc: Checks if this string view contains the pattern.

name: endsWith
desc: Checks whether the string view ends with the given pattern.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: indexOf
desc:
Returns the position of the first match of pattern in this view,
starting the search at start.

name: isEmpty
desc: Returns true if the string view is empty.

name: isNotEmpty
desc: Returns true if the string view is not empty.

name: length
desc: Returns the length of the string view.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: operator []
desc: Returns the character at the given index.

name: startsWith
desc: Checks whether the string view begins with the given pattern.

name: substring
desc: Returns a new StringView spanning a portion of this view.

name: toString
desc:
Instantiates a standard Dart String containing the precise view segment.
Note: This performs an $O(N)$ memory allocation!

name: trim
desc: Trims leading and trailing whitespace, returning a new tightly-bound StringView.

name: TakeRange

A view that yields up to the first count elements of an iterable.
Mimics C++ std::views::take.

desc: Creates a TakeRange that limits iteration to the first _count elements of _iterable.

Exposes the iterator from the underlying list.
This allows the Vector to be used in standard Dart for (var x in vec) loops.

You can overload the multiplication operator to take an int and return a new Vector repeated n times

name: operator ~

name: resize
desc:
Resizes the vector to contain count elements.
If the current size is greater than count, the container is reduced to its first count elements.
If the current size is less than count, the container is expanded by inserting copies of fill.

name: retainWhere

name: size

A view that iterates over two iterables in parallel, yielding Pairs.
